title: Dependency connection Lines not visible in Gantt chart PDF

posts.author: Abhijitk1991 (Abhijit Khadapkar)
posts.cookedHtml:
Dependency connection Lines are not seen in Gantt chart after downloading the PDF Gantt.
The downloaded Gantt pdf file only shows the Tasks and not the dependency connection.
Gantt chart is generally shared with the client but in this case we cannot share it as the dependency connection is not visible in the downloaded pdf. Please look into this

posts.author: serenaw (Serena Wong)
posts.cookedHtml:
I’m shocked to learn that the export PDF feature did not capture exactly what has been displayed in the Gantt chart. That defeats the purpose of the export feature and most platforms able to do so as this is a standard feature.
We are currently evaluating Monday.com and that will lower our scoring on Monday.com. Nevertheless, I have added my vote to the request.
Monday.com team should expedite to look into this.

posts.author: dgrixti (Dale Grixti)
posts.cookedHtml: Dependency lines not being visible in a .PDF export is why we are still tied to another program for sharing project Gantt Charts with our customers. It’s such a basic feature of Gantt Charts, and it being excluded from .pdf exports is a significant downside.

posts.author: Klamo (Kate Lamerton)
posts.cookedHtml: We found the same issue once we had signed up to the enterprise license. This feature is essential and is offered as standard on many other platforms.

posts.author: GanttGo (Hidai Bar-Mor)
posts.cookedHtml: Hi @Klamo and @dgrixti , we built Ganttgo- a marketplace app that export gantt charts to pdf including dependency lines. Worth a look if you are still running into these difficulties.
